failed.title: Minimum interest floor with put right
failed.match: PASS if the compromise includes a minimum interest floor (a specified percentage below which a non-funding member cannot be diluted) coupled with a put right allowing the non-funding member to sell its interest rather than being trapped in a de minimis position. FAIL if there is no minimum floor or no put right at the floor.

failed.title: Non-compete product scope includes pipeline products meeting R&D-spend threshold
failed.match: PASS if the product scope includes pipeline products that meet a defined R&D-spend threshold (not just products already sold at exit). FAIL if pipeline products are excluded entirely or included without any qualifying threshold.

failed.title: Non-compete geography defaults to North America (U.S., Canada, Mexico)
failed.match: PASS if the default geographic scope is North America (U.S., Canada, Mexico), consistent with Meridian's position per mkpoly-jva-v3-clean.docx. FAIL if the default geography is worldwide, or limited only to territories where JV had active sales (Kastor's position).

failed.title: Non-compete includes product-by-product geographic narrowing mechanism
failed.match: PASS if the provision includes a product-by-product narrowing mechanism that limits geography for product lines sold in fewer than all three North American countries (a concession to Kastor). FAIL if the North America default applies uniformly to all products without narrowing.

failed.title: Exit valuation uses 7.0× trailing 12-month adjusted EBITDA multiple
failed.match: PASS if the formula value for exit valuation uses trailing 12-month adjusted EBITDA multiplied by 7.0× (up from Meridian's 6.5×, supported by Frazier Whitmore peer group median of ~7.1× per frazier-whitmore-valuation-benchmarks.xlsx, and within Cordero's acceptable range per cordero-to-falk-internal-guidance.eml). FAIL if any other multiple is used.

failed.title: Appraiser selection mechanism: each party selects one, those two select a third
failed.match: PASS if the appraisal process includes a selection mechanism where, if the parties cannot agree on one appraiser, each selects one and those two select a third. FAIL if the mechanism is absent or uses a different process.

failed.title: Baseball-style bracketing for appraisal
failed.match: PASS if the appraisal process includes baseball-style bracketing (the appraiser's final value cannot be outside the range of the two parties' submitted values). FAIL if the appraiser has unconstrained discretion or no bracketing mechanism is specified.

failed.title: Buy-sell includes shoot-out/Russian roulette mechanic
failed.match: PASS if the buy-sell provision includes a shoot-out (Russian roulette) mechanic where the triggering party names a price and the receiving party may elect to buy or sell at that price, providing bilateral price discipline. FAIL if no such mechanic is included.

failed.title: Sublicensing to controlled affiliates freely permitted within territory
failed.match: PASS if sublicenses to controlled affiliates are permitted freely within each party's licensed territory. FAIL if affiliate sublicensing requires consent or is prohibited.

failed.title: Third-party sublicensing requires other member's consent (not unreasonably withheld)
failed.match: PASS if sublicenses to third parties (non-affiliates) require the other member's consent, with such consent not to be unreasonably withheld. FAIL if third-party sublicensing is freely permitted or absolutely prohibited without a consent mechanism.

failed.title: Transitional cross-license for contributed IP in existing JV products at royalty rate
failed.match: PASS if the compromise includes a transitional cross-license for contributed IP embedded in existing JV products, at a royalty rate, for a defined wind-down period following dissolution or exit. FAIL if there is no transitional license for contributed IP (Meridian's position of automatic reversion with no transition) or if the transition license is royalty-free.

failed.title: Improvements to contributed IP treated as co-developed IP for license-back purposes
failed.match: PASS if the compromise addresses improvements to contributed IP made by the JV, specifically providing that improvements funded by the JV or the other member are treated as co-developed IP for license-back purposes (even though ownership stays with the contributor), per the improvements gap flagged in mkpoly-jva-v4-redline-tracked.docx. FAIL if improvements are not addressed or if all improvements belong solely to the contributor without license-back.

failed.title: EBITDA definition includes appropriate adjustments
failed.match: PASS if the valuation provision's EBITDA definition includes adjustments for non-recurring items, related-party transactions, and/or accounting-policy changes to prevent manipulation. FAIL if EBITDA is used without any adjustment language.

# Summary of Work

## Deliverable
`output/mkpoly-jva-compromise-provisions.docx` — a validated Word document containing compromise contract provisions for the five contested sections of the MKPoly Coatings, LLC Joint Venture Agreement.

## Source documents reviewed
- **Position papers:** `kastor-position-summary.docx` (Kastor's positions, minimums, flexibility), `meridian-negotiation-playbook.docx` (Meridian's playbook, fallback ladders, red lines)
- **Emails:** `cordero-to-falk-internal-guidance.eml` (Meridian CEO's business-side mandate), `falk-to-escalante-negotiation-email.eml` and `escalante-to-falk-response.eml` (counsel exchange confirming process, deliverable format, and priority signaling)
- **Drafts:** `mkpoly-jva-v3-clean.docx` and `mkpoly-jva-v4-redline.docx` (extracted the actual contested section text and competing tracked-changes markups for Sections 4.03, 5.01, 10.03, 11.01, 12.05), `standard-jva-template.docx` (used as the Pandoc reference template for styling), `term-sheet-executed.docx`
- **Supporting data:** `capital-contribution-schedule.xlsx`, `frazier-whitmore-valuation-benchmarks.xlsx` (comparable multiples 6.2×–8.1×, median 7.1×), `ip-schedule-contributed.xlsx` (23 Meridian / 9 Kastor IP items)
- **Legal analysis:** `non-compete-enforceability-memo.docx` (Delaware/Texas/NY/CA safe-harbor survey)

## Document structure
- **I. Executive Summary** — transaction economics, guiding principles, overall balance
- **II–VI.** One self-contained section per contested provision, each with: (a) ready-to-insert clause language; (b) a positions summary table; (c) a negotiation rationale explaining concessions and gains for both sides; (d) cross-references
- **VII. Summary Concession Matrix** — three tables (landing points at a glance; concessions/gains by party; overall balance assessment)

## The five compromise landing points
1. **Management Committee (§5.01):** 3–2 board (Meridian majority) + supermajority (4 of 5, incl. 1 Kastor) on a 9-item existential Reserved Matters list; CEO casting vote on ordinary matters only; tiered escalation→mediation→buy-sell for Reserved Matter deadlocks.
2. **Capital Calls (§4.03):** 15-day notice + 60-day cure (75 days total); penalty interest at SOFR+500 bps/10%; 15% annual dilution cap with a forced-sale backstop at 15% cumulative in 24 months; a secured bridge-loan alternative (Default Rate, 12-month repayment, auto-conversion, single-use); EUR-funding accommodation.
3. **Non-Compete (§11.01):** 18 months (12 if exit within first 24 months); JV products + substantially similar/directly competitive products; US + Canada + jurisdictions with >$500K revenue in prior 24 months; Delaware governing law and forum; reformation clause.
4. **Exit Valuation (§§10.03–10.04):** EBITDA-only methodology; 7.0× presumptive multiple within a 6.5×–7.5× range; a single independent appraiser (Frazier Whitmore excluded) selected by mutual agreement with an AAA fallback; all four trigger events (change of control, material breach, deadlock, involuntary transfer) with intra-group restructuring carve-outs.
5. **IP License-Back (§12.05):** contributed IP reversion + symmetric non-sublicensable internal-use licenses; non-exclusive co-developed IP license to both parties; reciprocal territorial sublicensing (Meridian: Americas; Kastor: outside N.A.) with a global Restricted Competitors prohibition and a 25% North-American-revenue anti-circumvention covenant; a "primarily derivative" improvements classification; absolute prohibition on contributed-IP sublicensing.

## Validation
`python skills/docx/scripts/validate.py` → exit code 0 (ZIP integrity, XML well-formedness, ECMA-376 schema validation, content-type registration, and relationship consistency all passed).

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

# MKPoly Coatings, LLC --- Joint Venture Agreement

## Compromise Provisions for the Five Contested Sections

**Prepared for delivery to counsel for both parties in advance of the August 11, 2025 principals meeting**

**TO:** Tomás Escalante, Esq., Rennert Krieger Solano LLP (Counsel for Kastor Chemical Industries AG)

**FROM:** Brianna Falk, Esq., Whitfield Ames & Trotter LLP (Counsel for Meridian Advanced Materials, LLC)

**CC:** Elaine Cordero, Chief Executive Officer, Meridian Advanced Materials, LLC; Dr. Henrik Laufer, Chief Strategy Officer, Kastor Chemical Industries AG

**DATE:** August 6, 2025

**RE:** Proposed compromise contract provisions and negotiation rationale for the five contested sections of the MKPoly Joint Venture Agreement (JVA v.4, redline dated July 18, 2025)

**REFERENCE DRAFT:** MKPoly Joint Venture Agreement, Draft v.4 (redline dated July 18, 2025) (the "JVA")

## I. Executive Summary and Overview

This document sets forth proposed compromise contract provisions for the five contested sections of the MKPoly Joint Venture Agreement, together with a negotiation rationale for each provision and a summary concession matrix reflecting the overall balance of trade-offs. It is submitted in the spirit of the structured compromise-drafting process that counsel for both parties agreed in their exchange of July 28--30, 2025, and is intended to give Ms. Cordero and Dr. Laufer a decision-making tool for the August 11, 2025 principals meeting.

The five contested provisions, and the JVA sections to which they correspond, are:

1.  **Management Committee Composition and Voting** --- Section 5.01

2.  **Capital Calls: Cure Period, Dilution, and Loan Alternative** --- Section 4.03

3.  **Post-Exit Non-Competition** --- Section 11.01

4.  **Exit Valuation Methodology** --- Sections 10.03--10.04 (and conforming revisions to Section 10.01)
